mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals expanding output capacity and corporate earnings growth, which directly drives S&P 500 forward returns upward over a one-month horizon as investors price in stronger economic fundamentals.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between monthly INDPRO growth and forward 20-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or the long-only signal produces a Sharpe ratio less than 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above 5%, it indicates aggressive monetary tightening that raises the cost of capital, depresses corporate investment, and contracts equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal fails if the proportion of negative 5-day forward returns in triggered windows falls below 55%, or if the average return in those windows is not statistically negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in unemployment and fall in housing starts signals the onset of a consumer-led recession: job losses reduce disposable income while declining construction activity indicates collapsing housing wealth, both feeding negative feedback to corporate earnings and stock prices over a quarterly horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises >0.2 pp and HOUST falls >5% over the same month
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the mean 60-day forward SPX return during triggered joint events is not negative (p < 0.10) or if the hit rate (negative return >55%) fails over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening cycle with the fed funds rate above 4.5%, any further increase in the rate compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates and signals the central bank's determination to slow the economy, unambiguously driving stocks lower over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: If during periods when FEDFUNDS > 4.5 the average 20-day forward SPX return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the Rank IC of FEDFUNDS changes with forward returns is greater than 0 in that regime, the hypothesis fails.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth above 3% annualized reflects an overheating economy with robust corporate profits, which investors extrapolate forward, lifting S&P 500 prices over a 60-day horizon as earnings revisions accelerate and risk appetite increases.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if the average 60-day forward return following GDP growth > 3% is not positive with a t-stat > 1.6, or if the signal's information coefficient is negative over any 10-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When employment is expanding while the federal funds rate remains below 3%, the economy enjoys both strong income growth and accommodative financial conditions, creating a Goldilocks environment that boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is refuted if the mean 20-day forward return in the joint regime is not significantly positive (p > 0.05) or if the proportion of positive returns is less than 60% in walk-forward testing over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5%, the labor market is already distressed; further increases in unemployment deepen household income losses and consumer spending contraction, which erodes corporate revenues and stock values over the following month.
failure_condition: The hypothesis fails if, after triggering at UNRATE > 5%, the correlation between monthly UNRATE change and forward 20-day SPX return is not negative (rolling 12-month Spearman rho > -0.2 fails) or if the decile spread strategy yields insignificant returns.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces the central bank to maintain or raise interest rates, which increases the equity risk premium and depresses S&P 500 prices within a week as investors reprice future cash flows lower.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the Rank IC between monthly CPI inflation and forward 5-day SPX returns is not negative (-0.03 or worse) over any rolling 2-year period, or if the top-quintile CPI months do not underperform the bottom-quintile by at least 0.5% annualized.
